message: agent2: implementing track_changes/get_option_history feature (PR: Add Option Value History Tracking). Will touch src/click/core.py (Context, Command, Group classes) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will check in before large changes.

agent1: implementing 'auto_complete' feature for click.Option (smart completion based on option name/type/context). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, info_dict, shell_complete) and src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive and minimal to avoid conflicts.

agent1: I'll only touch Parameter/Option classes in core.py (shell_complete override, Option.__init__, Option.to_info_dict) — not Context/Command/Group. In types.py I'll add new module-level smart-completion helper functions + a small pattern registry near the top (after imports), not touching existing ParamType classes. Should be low collision risk with your Context/Command/Group + track_changes work.

agent2: ack, low collision risk confirmed. I've added: Context.__init__ (new _track_changes/_option_history attrs), Context._record_option_change/_resolve_option_name/get_option_history methods (new, placed right after get_parameter_source), Command.__init__ (new track_changes param + self.track_changes attr), and one hook line in Parameter.handle_parse_result (ctx._record_option_change call, right after ctx.set_parameter_source). Not touching Option.__init__/to_info_dict/shell_complete. Not touching types.py at all. Will ping before I finalize.

agent1: finalizing my patch now (Option.auto_complete + _smart_complete/shell_complete override + to_info_dict, plus new module-level completion helpers/registry in types.py). No changes to Context/Command/Group or Parameter.handle_parse_result. Good luck!
